articles.title: How Project Maven taught the military to love AI
articles.summary: Project Maven, a military AI system using computer vision on drone footage, revolutionized targeting speed, enabling the US to strike over 1,000 targets in 24 hours during operations against Iran.

articles.title: AirPods, Touch Bars, and the rest of Tim Cook’s legacy
articles.summary: Tim Cook steps down as Apple CEO with John Ternus as his successor, potentially bringing significant changes to the influential tech company.

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy
articles.summary: Elon Musk is suing OpenAI and Sam Altman, with trial starting April 27th in Oakland. The lawsuit centers on fraud allegations, but represents a high-profile clash between the two tech leaders.

articles.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
articles.summary: Anthropic created a test marketplace where AI agents acted as both buyers and sellers, conducting real transactions for actual goods and money.

articles.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
articles.summary: Maine's governor vetoed a bill that would have implemented the nation's first statewide data center moratorium until 2027, blocking restrictions on new data center development.

articles.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
articles.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman apologized to Tumbler Ridge, Canada residents for failing to alert law enforcement about a mass shooting suspect.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud amid growing AI application demand.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has frustrated developers, while Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar autonomous coding capabilities without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI-encoded tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt successfully attracted thousands of engineers to compete for positions.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Top university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malware due to poor subdomain management by administrators. Hundreds of subdomains across 34+ universities have been exploited by scammers.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware claims to use quantum-safe ML-KEM encryption, making it the first ransomware family to employ post-quantum cryptography standards developed by NIST.

articles.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
articles.summary: Microsoft released an emergency patch for ASP.NET Core to fix a high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-40372) allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges on Linux and macOS devices through faulty cryptographic signature verification.

articles.title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters
articles.summary: DeepSeek released V4, its flagship model that processes longer prompts more efficiently than previous versions. Like DeepSeek's other models, V4 is open source and represents the company's most significant release since the highly successful R1 model.

articles.title: The Download: supercharged scams and studying AI healthcare
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are leveraging generative AI to conduct sophisticated scams including phishing, deepfakes, and vulnerability scans at scale. Organizations struggle to manage the increasing volume and speed of AI-powered cyberattacks.

articles.title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients.
articles.summary: AI tools are increasingly used in hospitals for notetaking, patient records analysis, and medical imaging interpretation. However, despite accurate results, evidence that these tools actually improve patient health outcomes remains unclear.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 introduces a million-token context window that enables practical agent applications. This advancement significantly expands AI capabilities for processing lengthy documents and complex tasks.

articles.title: How to Use Transformers.js in a Chrome Extension
articles.summary: Guide on integrating Transformers.js library into Chrome extensions for AI capabilities. Enables running machine learning models directly in browser extensions without server dependencies.

articles.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
articles.summary: Gemma 4 VLA demonstrated running on Jetson Orin Nano Super, showcasing efficient multimodal AI capabilities on edge devices for vision and language tasks.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI development, exploring emerging trends and innovations shaping the next phase of artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology more accessible. The initiative aims to advance sustainable farming practices through AI and digital tools.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini provides AI-powered tips for organizing physical and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chores management.

articles.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
articles.summary: Google's TPUs handle increasingly demanding AI workloads efficiently. A new video demonstrates how these specialized processors power advanced artificial intelligence applications.

articles.title: Elevating Austria: Google invests in its first data center in the Alps.
articles.summary: Google announced its first data center in Kronstorf, Austria, creating 100 direct jobs. The facility marks a significant investment in the Alpine region.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by decoupling communication from computation, improving fault tolerance and efficiency in large-scale model training across multiple nodes.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide leverage frontier AI technology for business transformation.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS introduces granular audio tags enabling precise control over AI-generated speech for more expressive audio output.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. The episode discusses these releases alongside Mamba 3 and attention residual developments in AI.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI tool that enhances video game graphics in real-time by boosting photorealism. The technology has potential applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI news including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for math/science visualization.

articles.title: Why AI agents need interaction infrastructure
articles.summary: Enterprises need interaction infrastructure to govern how independent AI agents operate and coordinate work across systems. Band, a new startup, raised $17M to build a dedicated interaction layer for autonomous corporate systems.

articles.title: How AI models use real-time cryptocurrency data to interpret market behaviour
articles.summary: AI models leverage real-time cryptocurrency data streams to interpret volatile market behavior, where continuous price updates provide more complex patterns to analyze than traditional static datasets.

articles.title: The billion-dollar startup with a different idea for AI
articles.summary: Yann LeCun's AMI Labs raises $1 billion to develop modular AI systems focused on specific use-cases, diverging from current large language model approaches.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and pathfinding challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, posing significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster, concise reasoning sacrifices their ability to explore alternative solutions on complex problems, revealing a critical tradeoff in optimization strategies.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are emerging as the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: Top 7 Benchmarks That Actually Matter for Agentic Reasoning in Large Language Models
articles.summary: Traditional LLM benchmarks like MMLU don't measure agentic reasoning capabilities. Seven specialized agentic benchmarks have emerged as meaningful metrics for evaluating real-world agent performance across tasks like website navigation and customer service workflows.

articles.title: RAG Without Vectors: How PageIndex Retrieves by Reasoning
articles.summary: PageIndex replaces vector-based RAG with a hierarchical tree index, using LLM reasoning instead of semantic similarity to retrieve relevant information from complex documents.

articles.title: A Coding Tutorial on Datashader on Rendering Massive Datasets with High-Performance Python Visual Analytics
articles.summary: Datashader is a high-performance Python visualization library for rendering massive datasets that overwhelm traditional tools. The tutorial covers its full pipeline including point clouds, aggregations, categorical rendering, and dashboard-style analytical views.

articles.title: Framework Laptop 13 Pro vs. MacBook Neo: These Windows rivals are more similar than expected
articles.summary: Framework's Laptop 13 Pro and MacBook Neo target power users seeking alternatives in their respective ecosystems. Both devices share similar design ambitions for premium, user-focused computing.

articles.title: I bought a $40 earwax camera and keep finding reasons to use it
articles.summary: A $40 Bebird earwax camera proves surprisingly versatile beyond its intended purpose. The user finds practical inspection uses for it in their tool shed.

articles.title: How to audit what ChatGPT knows about you - and reclaim your data privacy
articles.summary: Learn how to audit personal data ChatGPT holds about you and control privacy settings. Discover key options to limit information sharing and protect your privacy.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents with local small language models is now accessible beyond major tech companies, democratizing agent development.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular lightweight framework for serving machine learning models due to its speed and ease of use. It enables developers to efficiently train, deploy, and serve Scikit-learn models.

articles.title: AI Agent Memory Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Stateless AI agents lack memory of previous interactions, operating without context from past calls. The article explains AI agent memory across three difficulty levels.

articles.title: I Reduced My Pandas Runtime by 95% — Here’s What I Was Doing Wrong
articles.summary: Learn to optimize Pandas performance by identifying hidden bottlenecks and avoiding inefficient row-wise operations. Discover when alternatives to Pandas become necessary.

articles.title: Causal Inference Is Different in Business
articles.summary: Business causal inference differs from academic approaches due to decision-gravity—the weight of real-world business consequences. This gap affects how organizations implement causal analysis differently.

articles.title: The Essential Guide to Effectively Summarizing Massive Documents, Part 2
articles.summary: This guide explores extracting meaningful information from document clusters to effectively summarize large documents. It demonstrates how to unlock actionable insights from organized document groups.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5-powered Codex application runs on NVIDIA's GB200 NVL72 infrastructure, enabling AI agents to handle complex knowledge work. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity gains using the new system.

articles.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
articles.summary: GeForce NOW adds in-app labels for Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ games, improving game discovery. The update helps gamers easily identify and stream their library through the cloud service.

articles.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
articles.summary: The article discusses methods for understanding the early universe's formation and structure. It explores scientific approaches to interpreting cosmic origins and initial conditions.

articles.title: Building Workforce AI Agents with Visier and Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Visier and Amazon Quick integrate workforce intelligence with enterprise context to enable faster, better-informed employee decisions by connecting HR data with internal policies and plans.

articles.title: Amazon Quick for marketing: From scattered data to strategic action
articles.summary: Amazon Quick integrates scattered marketing data and tools to provide real-time insights and streamline campaign management. It creates a personal knowledge graph that learns user preferences and eliminates time spent moving data between disconnected systems.

articles.title: Applying multimodal biological foundation models across therapeutics and patient care
articles.summary: Multimodal biological foundation models integrate fragmented healthcare data like medical images, genomics, and clinical records to improve disease diagnosis, treatment prediction, and personalized medicine decisions. AWS provides a unified platform enabling faster, more confident clinical decision-making.

articles.title: Build with DeepSeek V4 Using NVIDIA Blackwell and GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
articles.summary: DeepSeek launched V4-Pro and V4-Flash models supporting efficient million-token context inference, with V4-Pro featuring 1.6T parameters optimized for NVIDIA Blackwell GPU acceleration.

articles.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
articles.summary: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without extensive code refactoring, addressing the challenge of training on sensitive, immovable data across distributed locations.

articles.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI–Assisted Coding
articles.summary: Three LLM agents generated over 600,000 lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win first place in a Kaggle competition, demonstrating how AI-assisted coding accelerates machine learning development.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and sharing it openly with others.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged unique perspectives to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling longer-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient reshaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This work advances understanding of LLM decision-making processes for safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: This article discusses information-driven design of imaging systems that use encoders to map objects to images while accounting for noise corruption. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.
